introduction: The jury must decide whether a phased public policy pilot should expand nationwide, given disputed costs and benefits.

sentences.text: The opposition rejects the claim of net benefit; the pilot’s gains are anecdotal, not proven at scale.
Costs are understated and risks to vulnerable groups are being brushed aside for political convenience.
We affirm the policy improves outcomes for the majority when implemented with the safeguards we outline.
The pilot was voluntary, well-monitored, and showed measurable gains in participation and stability.
Expansion is the responsible next step, with funding tied to independent review milestones.
Opponents ignore twelve thousand households of evidence and independent fiscal review.
We propose phased rollout, strict eligibility audits, and a public dashboard so harm is visible early.
Rejecting expansion now would strand proven gains and surrender leadership on a solvable problem.

logicalFallacies.label: Straw man
logicalFallacies.description: Misrepresenting an argument to make it easier to attack.

logicalFallacies.label: Ad hominem
logicalFallacies.description: Attacking the person instead of the argument.

logicalFallacies.label: False dilemma
logicalFallacies.description: Presenting only two options when more exist.

sentences.text: Participation in the pilot was voluntary.
Sample size was twelve thousand households.
The interim report was published in March.
My opponent’s dismissal ignores the scale of the pilot; calling twelve thousand households “anecdotal” misstates what we measured.
The opposition frames our evidence as anecdotal, but the pilot covered twelve thousand voluntary households—hardly a stray sample.
That is a straw man: we claim phased, audited expansion, not blind faith in a single headline number.

logicalFallacies.label: Straw man
logicalFallacies.description: Misrepresenting an argument to make it easier to attack.

sentences.text: Twelve thousand voluntary participants are not anecdotes; they are the core dataset the March report summarizes.
To label that scale “unproven” while refusing comparable evidence on harm is a straw man—and it avoids the actual rollout safeguards we propose.

logicalFallacies.label: Straw man
logicalFallacies.description: Misrepresenting an argument to make it easier to attack.
